projectDescription: An industrial-grade C++ RPC framework supporting multiple protocols on the same port, designed for high-performance distributed systems at massive scale.
keyFeatures: Multi-protocol support on a single port (HTTP/H2, gRPC, Redis, Memcached, Thrift, and Baidu internal protocols)
High-performance M:N threading via bthread (user-space threads) for minimal latency
Flexible client patterns: synchronous, asynchronous, semi-synchronous, and combo channels
Built-in observability via HTTP-accessible services for profiling, tracing (rpcz), and metrics (bvar/vars)
Extensible architecture: custom naming services, load balancers, and protocols
Industrial-grade reliability used at Baidu with 1,000,000+ server instances
publicApiSurface: brpc::Server — server lifecycle and service registration
brpc::Channel — client-side RPC channel to a server or cluster
brpc::Controller — per-RPC parameters, metadata, and error handling
brpc::ClosureGuard — RAII helper to ensure done->Run() is called
brpc::ParallelChannel — fan-out requests to multiple sub-channels in parallel
brpc::SelectiveChannel — select one sub-channel per RPC for HA/sharding
brpc::IOBuf — zero-copy buffer for network I/O
